root.name: Add new text box on slide 1 below author name with required text
root.description: Evaluates whether the agent added a new text box on slide 1, placed it below the author name, and ensured the text inside exactly matches 'An Introduction to Flight Dynamics'.

children.name: A new text box is present on slide 1
children.description: Checks that a new text box was added to slide 1.

scorer.function_code:
def compute_score() -> tuple[str, float]:
    # Find new shapes on slide 1 in ppt_diff
    from pptx import Presentation
    from collections import Counter

    # Load presentations
    orig_prs = Presentation(original_ppt_path)
    mod_prs = Presentation(modified_ppt_path)
    # Get slide numbers mapping to ids
    def get_slide_by_id(prs):
        return {str(slide.slide_id): slide for slide in prs.slides}
    
    # Find added shapes
    # ppt_diff.added_slides: check slide_number==1
    # But for added shapes, need to compare element_count, or better: use PPTX
    orig_slide = orig_prs.slides[0]
    mod_slide = mod_prs.slides[0]
    orig_ids = set(shape.shape_id for shape in orig_slide.shapes)
    mod_ids = set(shape.shape_id for shape in mod_slide.shapes)
    added_ids = mod_ids - orig_ids
    added_shapes = [shape for shape in mod_slide.shapes if shape.shape_id in added_ids]
    # Filter for text boxes only (type == 'TEXT_BOX' or shape.has_text_frame)
    added_textboxes = [shape for shape in added_shapes if shape.has_text_frame]
    if added_textboxes:
        return "A new text box was added to slide 1.", 1.0
    else:
        return "No new text box found on slide 1.", 0.0

children.name: The text in the new text box matches 'An Introduction to Flight Dynamics'
children.description: Checks that the text in the new text box exactly matches 'An Introduction to Flight Dynamics'.

scorer.function_code:
def compute_score() -> tuple[str, float]:
    # Find new text boxes as before
    from pptx import Presentation
    orig_prs = Presentation(original_ppt_path)
    mod_prs = Presentation(modified_ppt_path)
    orig_slide = orig_prs.slides[0]
    mod_slide = mod_prs.slides[0]
    orig_ids = set(shape.shape_id for shape in orig_slide.shapes)
    mod_ids = set(shape.shape_id for shape in mod_slide.shapes)
    added_ids = mod_ids - orig_ids
    added_shapes = [shape for shape in mod_slide.shapes if shape.shape_id in added_ids]
    # Filter text boxes
    correct_text = 'An Introduction to Flight Dynamics'
    for shape in added_shapes:
        if shape.has_text_frame:
            shape_text = shape.text.strip()
            if shape_text == correct_text:
                return "New text box contains the required text.", 1.0
    return "No new text box contains the required text.", 0.0

children.name: The new text box is located below the author name
children.description: Checks that the new text box is positioned below the existing author name on slide 1 (vertical coordinate or visual layout).

scorer.function_code:
def compute_score() -> tuple[str, float]:
    # Use spatial relation if possible from pptx
    from pptx import Presentation
    correct_text = 'An Introduction to Flight Dynamics'
    orig_prs = Presentation(original_ppt_path)
    mod_prs = Presentation(modified_ppt_path)
    orig_slide = orig_prs.slides[0]
    mod_slide = mod_prs.slides[0]
    orig_textboxes = [shape for shape in orig_slide.shapes if shape.has_text_frame]
    mod_textboxes = [shape for shape in mod_slide.shapes if shape.has_text_frame]
    orig_ids = set(shape.shape_id for shape in orig_slide.shapes)
    mod_ids = set(shape.shape_id for shape in mod_slide.shapes)
    added_ids = mod_ids - orig_ids
    # Find author name text box (assume contains 'Sachin Karbari' or is the textbox just above added one)
    author_shape = None
    for shape in orig_textboxes:
        text = shape.text.strip().lower()
        if 'sachin karbari' in text:
            author_shape = shape
            break
    # Find new text box
    new_shape = None
    for shape in mod_textboxes:
        if shape.shape_id in added_ids:
            if shape.text.strip() == correct_text:
                new_shape = shape
                break
    if not author_shape or not new_shape:
        return "Could not reliably identify author textbox or new textbox.", 0.0
    # Check if new_shape is below author_shape
    if new_shape.top > author_shape.top:
        return "New text box is located below author name.", 1.0
    else:
        return "New text box is not located below author name.", 0.0

children.name: No extraneous modifications were made
children.description: Checks that no unrelated/unnecessary changes (added slides, extra text boxes, removal or modification of other content, etc.) were made.

scorer.function_code:
def compute_score() -> tuple[str, float]:
    # Check ppt_diff for unnecessary changes
    extraneous = []
    # Only added 1 textbox (with the correct text)
    from pptx import Presentation
    mod_prs = Presentation(modified_ppt_path)
    mod_slide = mod_prs.slides[0]
    mod_ids = set(shape.shape_id for shape in mod_slide.shapes if shape.has_text_frame)
    added_texts = [a.element_text for a in ppt_diff.added_slides if a.slide_id == 1]
    # Check added slides
    if ppt_diff.added_slides:
        extraneous.append("Slides were added.")
    # Check removed slides
    if ppt_diff.removed_slides:
        extraneous.append("Slides were removed.")
    # Check added/removed animations/transitions
    if ppt_diff.added_animations or ppt_diff.removed_animations or ppt_diff.added_transitions or ppt_diff.removed_transitions:
        extraneous.append("Animations or transitions were changed.")
    # Check removed or modified shapes on slide 1
    # It's hard to check modifications with ppt_diff reliably for shapes, so focus on additions/removals for this node
    # Did the agent add more than one text box to slide 1?
    orig_prs = Presentation(original_ppt_path)
    orig_slide = orig_prs.slides[0]
    orig_ids = set(shape.shape_id for shape in orig_slide.shapes if shape.has_text_frame)
    added_textboxes = mod_ids - orig_ids
    # Now count only those with the correct text
    from pptx import Presentation
    correct_text = 'An Introduction to Flight Dynamics'
    mod_prs = Presentation(modified_ppt_path)
    mod_slide = mod_prs.slides[0]
    correct_added = []
    for shape in mod_slide.shapes:
        if shape.shape_id in added_textboxes and shape.has_text_frame:
            if shape.text.strip() == correct_text:
                correct_added.append(shape)
    # If more than one added text box (with or without correct text), consider extraneous
    if len(added_textboxes) > len(correct_added):
        extraneous.append("Multiple text boxes were added.")
    # If no extraneous, score = 1
    if not extraneous:
        return "No extraneous modifications.", 1.0
    else:
        return f"Extraneous modifications found: {', '.join(extraneous)}", 0.0
